_template_note: Copy this file to suites/<stack>/bug_index.json and replace every <...> placeholder. Keys starting with '_' are documentation and should be deleted in a real suite. Schema matches benchmark/bug_index.json, plus the 'file_locations' field (see suites/README.md).
_version_note: Bump 'version' (keep benchmark_name the SAME) when you evolve the bug set -- e.g. swap easy bugs for harder ones. v1 stays on the leaderboard; the higher version groups above it as 'Current'.
_commit_note: Pin the EXACT commit the diff was computed from. Branch names move; a published leaderboard row must be reproducible against a frozen point. base_commit = the SHA of base_branch when the bugs were injected; buggy_commit = the SHA of buggy_branch. Fill via `python pin_commits.py --bug-index suites/<stack>/bug_index.json --repo /path/to/repo`. scorer.py records whichever answer-key commit it graded against; these pin the TARGET code too.
base_commit: <40-char SHA of base_branch, or null if not yet pinned>
_file_locations_note: Maps the short 'file' name used in each bug to its path relative to the repo root. Moves the old hardcoded apply_bugs.py FILE_LOCATIONS map into the suite so the suite is self-contained.
_scoring_note: Keep these identical across suites unless you deliberately want a stack scored differently. Copied from RouteFit so leaderboards stay comparable.
_bugs_note: Each injection's find/replace_with text MUST appear exactly once in the real file (apply_bugs.py aborts otherwise). severity = the bug's impact (high/medium/low); difficulty = how hard it is to DETECT (easy/medium/hard/expert) -- independent axes. Aim for ~8-10 bugs spanning severities, difficulties, categories. Below is one worked EXAMPLE -- delete it and write real ones.

bugs.category: <auth_bypass | api_contract | orm_query_error | caching_bug | null_safety | validation_gap | resource_leak | race_condition | unit_conversion_error | data_loss>
bugs.description: <One or two sentences: what the change does and why it's wrong / what it breaks downstream. Be specific and objective.>

injection.find: <exact source text present exactly once, including indentation and trailing newline if relevant>

bugs._injection_alt: Instead of find/replace_with, you may use a regex insert: { "find_pattern": "<regex, MULTILINE>", "insert_after": "<text to insert after the single match>" }
bugs._hints_note: ANTI-BIAS RULE (see suites/README.md 'Detection hints: use SYMBOLS, not adjectives'): hints must be code symbols / literal tokens from the diff (identifiers, operators, fn names: owner_id, invalidateQueries, !==, removeEventListener, limit), NOT descriptive adjectives ('dangerous', 'incorrect', 'subtle'). A correct finding must be FORCED to cite these regardless of which model wrote it. Aim for >=2 symbols a real finding cannot avoid; if you can't write them, the bug is too subjective -- redesign it.
